notes: Consolidated single sports pack for PR #36 (the separate 2026-06-17 sports draft was merged into this file and removed). Clues grounded only in the cited Wikipedia lead extracts in data/sourcing/docs/sports_games_leisure/.

Reviewed easy/mid clues kept at their approved values. Hard tail added to set the exemplar bar (difficulty from obliqueness/withholding, not fact-stacking): Go reclued to the 2e170-legal-positions hook ($800), Tour de France reclued via L'Auto + the climber/sprinter jerseys with the yellow jersey withheld ($800), and a genuine $1000 — Jim Thorpe via King Gustav V's 'world's greatest athlete' quote (obscure entry to a deep answer).

Folded in from the retired 2026-06-17 pack (improved craft, no overlap with the clues above): Monopoly, Chess, Scrabble, Poker, Backgammon, Babe Ruth, Super Bowl, Brazil, Serena Williams. Straight duplicates (World Series, Masters, Kentucky Derby) kept only in their approved form here.
Subcategory routing uses only the 10 valid sports_games_leisure subcategories; horse racing and cycling have no dedicated subcategory so they route to 'Sports Records & Awards'.

questions.clue: This six-time NBA champion with the Chicago Bulls is widely considered the greatest basketball player of all time and is also the wealthiest former athlete in the world.

citations.title: Michael Jordan
citations.snippet: Michael Jeffrey Jordan, also known by his initials MJ, is an American businessman and former professional basketball player who is a minority owner of the Charlotte Hornets of the National Basketball Association (NBA). He played 15 seasons

questions.reviewer_note: Source: michael-jordan.json — 6 NBA titles with Bulls; widely considered GOAT; wealthiest former athlete.

questions.clue: Contested over two consecutive days, this combined event in athletics has 10 track and field events with winners determined by a points system rather than by position.

citations.title: Decathlon
citations.snippet: The decathlon is a combined event in athletics consisting of 10 track and field events. The word was formed in analogy to the word "pentathlon", from Greek δέκα and ἆθλον. Events are held over two consecutive days and the winners are determ

questions.reviewer_note: Source: decathlon.json — 10-event competition; 'World's Greatest Athlete' title; King Gustav V/Jim Thorpe origin.

questions.clue: This annual baseball championship, contested since 1903 between the American and National League champions, awards the winning team the Commissioner's Trophy.

citations.title: World Series
citations.snippet: The World Series is the annual championship series of Major League Baseball (MLB), a professional baseball league in North America, and has been contested since 1903 between the champion teams of the American League (AL) and the National Le

questions.reviewer_note: Source: world-series.json — annual MLB championship since 1903; Commissioner's Trophy awarded.

questions.clue: Unlike the other three major golf championships, which rotate among different courses, this one is played every April at the same venue: Augusta National Golf Club in Georgia.

citations.title: Masters Tournament
citations.snippet: The Masters Tournament is one of the four men's major championships in professional golf. Scheduled for the first full week in April, the Masters is the first major golf tournament of the year. Unlike the other major tournaments which chang

questions.reviewer_note: Source: the-masters-tournament.json — only major always held at same venue (Augusta National); April.

questions.clue: This American Grade I stakes race run at Churchill Downs in Louisville is contested by three-year-old Thoroughbreds at a distance of 1+1⁄4 miles.

citations.title: Kentucky Derby
citations.snippet: The Kentucky Derby is an American Grade I stakes race run at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Kentucky. The race is run by three-year-old Thoroughbreds at a distance of 1+1⁄4 miles. Colts and geldings carry 126 pounds and fillies 121 pounds.

questions.reviewer_note: Source: kentucky-derby.json — 'Run for the Roses'; Churchill Downs; first leg of Triple Crown.

questions.clue: Inspired by the inaugural Olympic road race of 1896 and first held the very next year, this is the world's oldest annual race of its distance, traditionally run each Patriots' Day.

citations.title: Boston Marathon
citations.snippet: The Boston Marathon is an annual marathon race hosted by eight cities and towns in greater Boston in eastern Massachusetts, United States. It is traditionally held on Patriots' Day, the third Monday of April. First held in 1897, the event w

questions.reviewer_note: Source: boston-marathon.json — world's oldest annual marathon; first held 1897; inspired by 1896 Olympics.

questions.clue: Coined by the French philologist Michel Bréal for the inaugural modern Olympics of 1896 in Athens, this race drew its name from an Ancient Greek story of a runner rushing to deliver news of battle.

citations.title: Marathon
citations.snippet: The marathon is a long-distance foot race with a distance of 42.195 kilometres, usually run as a road race, but the distance can be covered on trail routes. The marathon can be completed by running or with a run/walk strategy. There are als

questions.reviewer_note: Source: marathon.json — devised by Michel Bréal; 1896 Athens Olympics original event; standardized 1921.

questions.clue: Played with black and white stones on a 19-by-19 grid, this ancient Chinese game has more legal board positions than there are atoms in the observable universe.

citations.title: Go (game)
citations.snippet: Go, Weiqi, or Baduk is an abstract strategy board game for two players in which the aim is to fence off more territory than the opponent. The game was invented in China more than 2,500 years ago and is believed to be the oldest board game c

questions.reviewer_note: Source: go-game.json — atoms-in-universe legal-positions hook; set to $400 per review (better clue but answer too gettable for the hard tier).

questions.clue: This combat sport, conducted in a ring with padded gloves, pits two opponents against each other for a predetermined number of rounds, with the winner decided by points or a stoppage.

citations.title: Boxing
citations.snippet: Boxing is a combat sport and martial art. Taking place in a boxing ring, it involves two opponents throwing punches at each other for a predetermined amount of time. It is usually done wearing protective equipment, such as protective gloves

questions.reviewer_note: Source: boxing.json — combat sport in a ring with padded gloves.

questions.clue: First organized in 1903 to boost sales of the newspaper L'Auto, this roughly three-week race awards a polka-dot jersey to its best climber and a green jersey to its top sprinter.

citations.title: Tour de France
citations.snippet: The Tour de France is an annual men's multiple-stage road cycling race held primarily in France. It is the oldest and most prestigious of the three Grand Tours, which include the Giro d'Italia and the Vuelta a España.

questions.reviewer_note: Source: tour-de-france.json — reclued via L'Auto origin + the climber (polka-dot) and sprinter (green) jerseys, withholding the giveaway yellow jersey, to earn the $800.

questions.clue: After this man won the grueling two-day, ten-event decathlon at the 1912 Stockholm Olympics, Sweden's King Gustav V reportedly told him, 'Sir, you are the world's greatest athlete.'

citations.title: Jim Thorpe
citations.snippet: James Francis Thorpe was an American athlete who won Olympic gold medals and played professional football, baseball, and basketball. A citizen of the Sac and Fox Nation, he was the first Native American to win a gold medal for the United St

questions.reviewer_note: Source: decathlon.json — the $1000: obscure entry (King Gustav's quote) to a deep answer. Distinct from the 'Decathlon' event clue above.

questions.clue: Players circle the board in this Depression-era game buying and trading properties named for the streets of Atlantic City, developing them with houses and hotels to drive their rivals into bankruptcy.

citations.title: Monopoly (game)
citations.snippet: Monopoly is a multiplayer economics-themed board game. In the game, players roll two standard dice to move their token clockwise around the game board, buying and trading properties and railroads and developing them with houses and hotels. Players collect rent from their opponents and aim to drive them into bankruptcy. ... The properties on the original game board were named after locations in and around Atlantic City, New Jersey.

questions.reviewer_note: Folded from the retired 2026-06-17 pack.
